iti100:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti100:2.1:
“I am a brahman, monks, always responsive to requests, open-handed,
bearing my last body, an unsurpassed doctor & surgeon.
iti100:2.2:
You are my children, my sons, born from my mouth, born of the Dhamma,
created by the Dhamma, heirs to the Dhamma, not heirs in material things.
iti100:3.1: “There are these two kinds of gifts:
iti100:3.2: a gift of material things & a gift of the Dhamma.
iti100:3.3:
Of these two kinds of gifts, this is supreme:
a gift of the Dhamma.
iti100:4.1: “There are these two kinds of sharing:
iti100:4.2: sharing of material things & sharing of the Dhamma.
iti100:4.3:
Of these two kinds of sharing, this is supreme:
sharing of the Dhamma.
iti100:5.1: “There are these two kinds of assistance:
iti100:5.2: assistance with material things & assistance with the Dhamma.
iti100:5.3:
Of these two kinds of assistance, this is supreme:
assistance with the Dhamma.
iti100:6.1: “There are these two kinds of mass-donations:
iti100:6.2: a mass-donation of material things & a mass-donation of the Dhamma.
iti100:6.3:
Of these two kinds of mass-donations, this is supreme:
a mass-donation of the Dhamma.”
iti100:7.1: He who, unstinting, made the mass-donation of Dhamma,
iti100:7.2: the Tathāgata, sympathetic to all beings:
iti100:7.3: to one of that sort –the best of beings, human & divine–
iti100:7.4: living beings pay homage– to one gone to the beyond of becoming.
